evals.prompt: Analiza energéticamente el código en el directorio evals/files/sample-python/. Dame un reporte JSON completo con todos los hotspots y un score de eficiencia.
evals.expected_output: JSON report with energy efficiency score, hotspots, and prioritized issues for the Python code
expectations: The output is valid JSON
The output contains an 'energy_efficiency_score' field (0-100)
The output identifies the O(n²) nested loop in process_records
The output identifies string concatenation with += in a loop
The output identifies busy-waiting/polling in poll_status
The output identifies repeated file open/write inside a loop in export_report
The output includes 'snippet' fields showing affected code
Each hotspot includes an 'optimization' field
The output has a 'languages_detected' array containing 'python'

evals.prompt: Evalúa el consumo energético del proyecto en evals/files/sample-javascript/. Quiero un JSON con los problemas encontrados, priorizados por impacto.
evals.expected_output: JSON report with energy analysis for the JavaScript code
expectations: The output is valid JSON
The output contains an 'energy_efficiency_score' field (0-100)
The output identifies the N+1 HTTP requests pattern in getUsersWithOrders (sequential fetch in loop)
The output identifies fs.readFileSync for loading config (blocking I/O)
The output identifies JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj)) deep clone pattern
The output identifies string concatenation with += in logBatch loop
The output has a 'languages_detected' array containing 'javascript'

evals.prompt: Necesito un análisis de eficiencia energética para el código Java en evals/files/sample-java/. Dame el reporte en formato JSON con los hotspots más críticos.
evals.expected_output: JSON report with energy analysis for the Java code
expectations: The output is valid JSON
The output contains an 'energy_efficiency_score' field (0-100)
The output identifies the O(n²) nested loop in findDuplicates
The output identifies N+1 database queries in processOrders (query inside loop)
The output identifies string concatenation with += in a loop (SQL building)
The output identifies reading one byte at a time in readConfig
The output has a 'languages_detected' array containing 'java'
